session_notes.note: Honestly, I don't know why I'm even here. I've tried quitting before, and it never works out. I've been smoking for 20 years, and I just don't see the point in trying again.

I don't know... I just couldn't do it. I tried nicotine gum, prescription medication, and even cold turkey. But I always ended up going back to smoking. And it's not just that... I'm worried about gaining weight. I've always been conscious of my weight, and the thought of gaining even a few pounds is unbearable to me.
I'm not sure... I've tried to diet before, and it never works out. And besides, I don't think I'm ready to quit smoking right now. I'm just not in the right mindset.
Okay, fine. Let's get this over with. But like I said, I'm not making any promises.
Honestly, I don't know why I'm here. I'm not really interested in quitting. I've tried before, and it never works out. I've been smoking for 20 years, and it's just a part of who I am.

Well, I've tried nicotine gum, patches, and even cold turkey. But every time, I end up feeling anxious and depressed. It's like my mental health gets worse when I try to quit. And on top of that, I always gain weight when I quit, which is really frustrating.

Yeah, I've talked to my doctor. They just tell me to quit smoking, like it's that easy. They don't understand how hard it is. And my family and friends don't get it either. They're all smokers, so they just think I should keep smoking and not worry about it.
